mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2.matched_phenotypes: psychosis, hematochezia, hypomagnesemia, limb muscle weakness
renal abnormalities, angiokeratoma (facial nevi), varicose veins, hypomagnesaemia
facial neoplasms, seizures (psychosis can be a symptom)
rhonchi (wheezing sounds heard during breathing), cafe au lait spots (not mentioned but common in neurofibromatosis)
recurrent infections, tubular protein loss leading to hypomagnesium and phosphate wasting
hypokalemic alkalosis with normal renin levels, dehydration causing constipation, and secondary hyperaldosteronism resulting in hypertension and hematuria
characteristic facies, connective tissue disorders including varicosities, growth retardation, and susceptibility to bacterial infections
paroxysmal episodes of headache, sweating, palpitations, anxiety, and gastrointestinal symptoms such as diarrhea or hematochezia
joint hypermobility, easy bruising, and vascular fragility that may lead to hemorrhoids
bone pain, fracture proneness, short stature, and calcium oxalate urolithiasis
